In [19]:
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# §8e  MULTIVARIABLE FATIGUE MODELS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
import statsmodels.api as sm
from statsmodels.stats.outliers_influence import variance_inflation_factor

# Outcome: y_score_ensemble  (AD-likeness)
# Primary predictor: FAS_ordinal
# Confounders: Age, Sex, COVID severity, recovery

_model_data = fat_valid.copy()

# Encode confounders
if "Sex" in _model_data.columns:
    _model_data["Sex_M"] = (_model_data["Sex"] == "M").astype(float)

catcovid_col_local = None
for c in ["CategoríaCOVID", "CategoriaCOVID"]:
    if c in _model_data.columns:
        catcovid_col_local = c; break
if catcovid_col_local:
    _sev_map = {"No infectado": 0, "Paciente ambulatorio": 1,
                "Internación moderada": 2, "Internación severa (UCI)": 3}
    _model_data["severity_ord"] = _model_data[catcovid_col_local].map(_sev_map)

if "Recuperado" in _model_data.columns:
    _rec_map = {"completamente": 0, "parcialmente": 1, "No mucho": 2, "para nada": 3}
    _model_data["recovery_ord"] = _model_data["Recuperado"].map(_rec_map)

# Build predictor list
_pred_cols = ["FAS_ordinal", "Age"]
if "Sex_M" in _model_data.columns:
    _pred_cols.append("Sex_M")
if "severity_ord" in _model_data.columns and _model_data["severity_ord"].notna().sum() > 20:
    _pred_cols.append("severity_ord")
if "recovery_ord" in _model_data.columns and _model_data["recovery_ord"].notna().sum() > 20:
    _pred_cols.append("recovery_ord")

# Complete cases
_reg_cols = ["y_score_ensemble"] + _pred_cols
_avail = [c for c in _reg_cols if c in _model_data.columns]
_cc = _model_data[_avail].dropna()

print(f"§8e — Multivariable model: y_score_ensemble ~ {' + '.join(_pred_cols)}")
print(f"Complete cases: N = {len(_cc)}\n")

if len(_cc) < 20:
    print("⚠ Too few complete cases. Skipping multivariable model.")
else:
    y = _cc["y_score_ensemble"]
    X = _cc[[c for c in _pred_cols if c in _cc.columns]]

    # Standardise for interpretable coefficients
    X_std = (X - X.mean()) / X.std()
    X_std = sm.add_constant(X_std)

    model = sm.OLS(y, X_std).fit(cov_type="HC3")
    print(f"R² = {model.rsquared:.4f},  adj-R² = {model.rsquared_adj:.4f}\n")
    print(model.summary2().tables[1].to_string())

    # VIF
    _vif_data = sm.add_constant(X)
    if _vif_data.shape[1] > 2:
        print("\nVariance Inflation Factors:")
        for i, col in enumerate(_vif_data.columns):
            if col == "const": continue
            vif = variance_inflation_factor(_vif_data.values, i)
            print(f"  {col:20s}: VIF = {vif:.2f}")

    # Also model S_sig as outcome
    if "S_sig" in _model_data.columns:
        _cc2 = _model_data[["S_sig"] + _pred_cols].dropna()
        if len(_cc2) > 20:
            y2 = _cc2["S_sig"]
            X2 = _cc2[[c for c in _pred_cols if c in _cc2.columns]]
            X2_std = (X2 - X2.mean()) / X2.std()
            X2_std = sm.add_constant(X2_std)
            m2 = sm.OLS(y2, X2_std).fit(cov_type="HC3")
            print(f"\n--- S_sig ~ {' + '.join(_pred_cols)} ---")
            print(f"N = {len(_cc2)}, R² = {m2.rsquared:.4f}")
            if "FAS_ordinal" in m2.params.index:
                _b = m2.params["FAS_ordinal"]
                _p = m2.pvalues["FAS_ordinal"]
                print(f"FAS_ordinal: β = {_b:.4f}, p = {_p:.4f}")

    # Also model recon_error_ensemble
    if "recon_error_ensemble" in _model_data.columns:
        _cc3 = _model_data[["recon_error_ensemble"] + _pred_cols].dropna()
        if len(_cc3) > 20:
            y3 = _cc3["recon_error_ensemble"]
            X3 = _cc3[[c for c in _pred_cols if c in _cc3.columns]]
            X3_std = (X3 - X3.mean()) / X3.std()
            X3_std = sm.add_constant(X3_std)
            m3 = sm.OLS(y3, X3_std).fit(cov_type="HC3")
            print(f"\n--- recon_error ~ {' + '.join(_pred_cols)} ---")
            print(f"N = {len(_cc3)}, R² = {m3.rsquared:.4f}")
            if "FAS_ordinal" in m3.params.index:
                _b = m3.params["FAS_ordinal"]
                _p = m3.pvalues["FAS_ordinal"]
                print(f"FAS_ordinal: β = {_b:.4f}, p = {_p:.4f}")

§8e — Multivariable model: y_score_ensemble ~ FAS_ordinal + Age + Sex_M + severity_ord + recovery_ord
Complete cases: N = 145

R² = 0.0284,  adj-R² = -0.0065

                 Coef.  Std.Err.          z          P>|z|    [0.025    0.975]
const         0.372932  0.012326  30.254689  4.526527e-201  0.348773  0.397092
FAS_ordinal   0.006571  0.013964   0.470605   6.379229e-01 -0.020797  0.033940
Age          -0.003210  0.013532  -0.237209   8.124945e-01 -0.029732  0.023312
Sex_M         0.020863  0.014139   1.475587   1.400547e-01 -0.006849  0.048575
severity_ord  0.001874  0.013449   0.139346   8.891768e-01 -0.024485  0.028233
recovery_ord -0.012899  0.013124  -0.982818   3.256971e-01 -0.038621  0.012824

Variance Inflation Factors:
  FAS_ordinal         : VIF = 1.12
  Age                 : VIF = 1.05
  Sex_M               : VIF = 1.15
  severity_ord        : VIF = 1.13
  recovery_ord        : VIF = 1.06

--- S_sig ~ FAS_ordinal + Age + Sex_M + severity_ord + recovery_ord ---
N = 145, R² = 0.0937
FAS_ordinal: β = 2.2061, p = 0.0186

--- recon_error ~ FAS_ordinal + Age + Sex_M + severity_ord + recovery_ord ---
N = 145, R² = 0.0277
FAS_ordinal: β = -0.0044, p = 0.2924
In [20]:
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# §8f  EXTREME-VS-NONE FOCUSED CONTRAST
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

from scipy.stats import mannwhitneyu

_g_none = fat_valid[fat_valid["CategoriaFAS"] == "NO HAY FATIGA"]
_g_ext  = fat_valid[fat_valid["CategoriaFAS"] == "FATIGA EXTREMA"]

print(f"§8f — FATIGA EXTREMA (n={len(_g_ext)}) vs NO HAY FATIGA (n={len(_g_none)})")
print("=" * 60)

_evn_vars = ["y_score_ensemble", "y_score_std", "recon_error_ensemble",
             "S_sig", "MOCA", "MOCA_perc", "EQ-VAS"]
_evn_vars = [v for v in _evn_vars if v in fat_valid.columns]

evn_rows = []
for var in _evn_vars:
    v1 = _g_ext[var].dropna().values
    v2 = _g_none[var].dropna().values
    if len(v1) < 3 or len(v2) < 3:
        continue
    U, p = mannwhitneyu(v1, v2, alternative="two-sided")
    n1, n2 = len(v1), len(v2)
    delta = 2 * U / (n1 * n2) - 1  # Cliff's delta

    # Bootstrap CI for Cliff's delta
    rng = np.random.RandomState(SEED_GLOBAL)
    boot_deltas = []
    for _ in range(N_BOOTSTRAP):
        _b1 = rng.choice(v1, len(v1), replace=True)
        _b2 = rng.choice(v2, len(v2), replace=True)
        _bU, _ = mannwhitneyu(_b1, _b2, alternative="two-sided")
        boot_deltas.append(2 * _bU / (n1 * n2) - 1)
    ci_lo = np.percentile(boot_deltas, 2.5)
    ci_hi = np.percentile(boot_deltas, 97.5)

    evn_rows.append({
        "variable": var,
        "median_EXTREMA": np.median(v1),
        "median_NONE": np.median(v2),
        "n_EXTREMA": n1, "n_NONE": n2,
        "cliffs_delta": delta,
        "delta_CI_lo": ci_lo, "delta_CI_hi": ci_hi,
        "p_raw": p,
    })

evn_df = pd.DataFrame(evn_rows)
if len(evn_df) > 0:
    _, q_vals, _, _ = multipletests(evn_df["p_raw"], method="fdr_bh")
    evn_df["q_fdr"] = q_vals
    evn_df["sig_fdr05"] = q_vals < 0.05

    print("\nExtreme-vs-None contrast (BH-FDR corrected):\n")
    print(evn_df.to_string(index=False, float_format="{:.4f}".format))
    evn_df.to_csv(TABLES_DIR / "fatigue_extreme_vs_none.csv", index=False)

    # Effect-size interpretation
    print("\nEffect-size interpretation (Cliff's δ):")
    for _, r in evn_df.iterrows():
        d = abs(r["cliffs_delta"])
        if d < 0.11:
            mag = "negligible"
        elif d < 0.28:
            mag = "small"
        elif d < 0.43:
            mag = "medium"
        else:
            mag = "large"
        sig = "★" if r["sig_fdr05"] else ""
        print(f"  {r['variable']:25s}: δ={r['cliffs_delta']:+.3f} "
              f"[{r['delta_CI_lo']:+.3f}, {r['delta_CI_hi']:+.3f}] "
              f"({mag}) {sig}")

§8f — FATIGA EXTREMA (n=29) vs NO HAY FATIGA (n=24)
============================================================

Extreme-vs-None contrast (BH-FDR corrected):

            variable  median_EXTREMA  median_NONE  n_EXTREMA  n_NONE  cliffs_delta  delta_CI_lo  delta_CI_hi  p_raw  q_fdr  sig_fdr05
    y_score_ensemble          0.3817       0.4142         29      24       -0.0690      -0.3851       0.2443 0.6746 0.9444      False
         y_score_std          0.0587       0.0643         29      24       -0.1466      -0.4684       0.1811 0.3669 0.6420      False
recon_error_ensemble          0.5176       0.5234         29      24       -0.1494      -0.4483       0.1753 0.3575 0.6420      False
               S_sig         -1.7550      -8.6222         29      24        0.3161       0.0144       0.6092 0.0504 0.1764      False
                MOCA         26.0000      26.5000         29      24       -0.0345      -0.3606       0.2874 0.8357 0.9750      False
           MOCA_perc          0.5000       0.6650         29      24        0.0029      -0.3132       0.3147 0.9929 0.9929      False
              EQ-VAS         55.0000      80.0000         29      24       -0.8362      -0.9670      -0.6595 0.0000 0.0000       True

Effect-size interpretation (Cliff's δ):
  y_score_ensemble         : δ=-0.069 [-0.385, +0.244] (negligible) 
  y_score_std              : δ=-0.147 [-0.468, +0.181] (small) 
  recon_error_ensemble     : δ=-0.149 [-0.448, +0.175] (small) 
  S_sig                    : δ=+0.316 [+0.014, +0.609] (medium) 
  MOCA                     : δ=-0.034 [-0.361, +0.287] (negligible) 
  MOCA_perc                : δ=+0.003 [-0.313, +0.315] (negligible) 
  EQ-VAS                   : δ=-0.836 [-0.967, -0.659] (large) ★
In [21]:
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# §8g  SENSITIVITY ANALYSES  (fatigue)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

from scipy.stats import spearmanr, mannwhitneyu, kruskal

print("§8g — Sensitivity / robustness checks\n")

_fas_order = ["NO HAY FATIGA", "FATIGA", "FATIGA EXTREMA"]
_main_var = "y_score_ensemble"
_sens_results = []

# ── 1. Ambulatory-only subset ──
if catcovid_col_local and catcovid_col_local in fat_valid.columns:
    _amb = fat_valid[fat_valid[catcovid_col_local] == "Paciente ambulatorio"]
    if len(_amb) > 10 and _amb["CategoriaFAS"].nunique() >= 2:
        _grps = [_amb.loc[_amb["CategoriaFAS"] == g, _main_var].dropna().values
                 for g in _fas_order]
        _grps = [g for g in _grps if len(g) >= 3]
        if len(_grps) >= 2:
            H, p = kruskal(*_grps) if len(_grps) >= 3 else mannwhitneyu(_grps[0], _grps[1])
            print(f"  1. Ambulatory-only (n={len(_amb)}): "
                  f"{'KW' if len(_grps)>=3 else 'MWU'} p = {p:.4f}")
            _sens_results.append({"check": "ambulatory_only",
                                  "n": len(_amb), "p": p})

# ── 2. Excluding ICU (tiny stratum) ──
if catcovid_col_local:
    _no_icu = fat_valid[fat_valid[catcovid_col_local] != "Internación severa (UCI)"]
    if len(_no_icu) > 10 and _no_icu["CategoriaFAS"].nunique() >= 2:
        rho, p = spearmanr(_no_icu["FAS_ordinal"].dropna(),
                           _no_icu.loc[_no_icu["FAS_ordinal"].notna(), _main_var])
        print(f"  2. Excluding ICU (n={len(_no_icu)}): "
              f"Spearman ρ = {rho:.4f}, p = {p:.4f}")
        _sens_results.append({"check": "excl_ICU", "n": len(_no_icu),
                              "rho": rho, "p": p})

# ── 3. Sex-stratified ──
if "Sex" in fat_valid.columns:
    for sex in ["M", "F"]:
        _ss = fat_valid[fat_valid["Sex"] == sex]
        if len(_ss) > 10 and _ss["FAS_ordinal"].notna().sum() > 10:
            rho, p = spearmanr(_ss["FAS_ordinal"].dropna(),
                               _ss.loc[_ss["FAS_ordinal"].notna(), _main_var])
            print(f"  3. Sex={sex} only (n={len(_ss)}): "
                  f"Spearman ρ = {rho:.4f}, p = {p:.4f}")
            _sens_results.append({"check": f"sex_{sex}_only",
                                  "n": len(_ss), "rho": rho, "p": p})

# ── 4. Controls included (secondary check) ──
_with_ctrl = fat_all[fat_all["CategoriaFAS"].isin(_fas_order)].copy()
if "y_score_ensemble" not in _with_ctrl.columns:
    _ens_tmp = ensemble[ensemble["classifier"] == TARGET_CLF].copy()
    _with_ctrl = _with_ctrl.merge(
        _ens_tmp[["SubjectID", "y_score_ensemble"]],
        on="SubjectID", how="left")
_with_ctrl["FAS_ordinal"] = _with_ctrl["CategoriaFAS"].map(
    {"NO HAY FATIGA": 0, "FATIGA": 1, "FATIGA EXTREMA": 2})
_valid_wc = _with_ctrl[["FAS_ordinal", "y_score_ensemble"]].dropna()
if len(_valid_wc) > 20:
    rho, p = spearmanr(_valid_wc["FAS_ordinal"], _valid_wc["y_score_ensemble"])
    print(f"  4. All (COVID+CONTROL, n={len(_valid_wc)}): "
          f"Spearman ρ = {rho:.4f}, p = {p:.4f}")
    _sens_results.append({"check": "all_incl_ctrl", "n": len(_valid_wc),
                          "rho": rho, "p": p})

# ── 5. EQ-VAS as alternative outcome ──
if "EQ-VAS" in fat_valid.columns:
    _eq = fat_valid[["FAS_ordinal", "EQ-VAS"]].dropna()
    if len(_eq) > 10:
        rho, p = spearmanr(_eq["FAS_ordinal"], _eq["EQ-VAS"])
        print(f"  5. EQ-VAS vs FAS ordinal (n={len(_eq)}): "
              f"Spearman ρ = {rho:.4f}, p = {p:.4f}")
        _sens_results.append({"check": "EQVAS_vs_FAS",
                              "n": len(_eq), "rho": rho, "p": p})

# Save
if _sens_results:
    pd.DataFrame(_sens_results).to_csv(
        TABLES_DIR / "fatigue_sensitivity_analyses.csv", index=False)
    print(f"\n  Saved → fatigue_sensitivity_analyses.csv")

print("\n✓ Sensitivity analyses complete.")

§8g — Sensitivity / robustness checks

  1. Ambulatory-only (n=130): KW p = 0.6605
  2. Excluding ICU (n=146): Spearman ρ = -0.0343, p = 0.6810
  3. Sex=M only (n=45): Spearman ρ = 0.0193, p = 0.8998
  3. Sex=F only (n=105): Spearman ρ = -0.0051, p = 0.9585
  4. All (COVID+CONTROL, n=193): Spearman ρ = -0.0528, p = 0.4655
  5. EQ-VAS vs FAS ordinal (n=150): Spearman ρ = -0.4704, p = 0.0000

  Saved → fatigue_sensitivity_analyses.csv

✓ Sensitivity analyses complete.
In [22]:
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# §8h  FATIGUE FIGURES (publication-quality)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

_fas_order = ["NO HAY FATIGA", "FATIGA", "FATIGA EXTREMA"]
_fas_palette = {"NO HAY FATIGA": "#2ECC71", "FATIGA": "#F39C12",
                "FATIGA EXTREMA": "#E74C3C"}

# ── Figure 1: AD-likeness by CategoriaFAS (violin + strip) ──
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(17, 5.5))

# Panel A: AD-likeness score
ax = axes[0]
_pdata = fat_valid[fat_valid["CategoriaFAS"].isin(_fas_order)].copy()
_pdata["CategoriaFAS"] = pd.Categorical(_pdata["CategoriaFAS"],
                                         categories=_fas_order, ordered=True)
sns.violinplot(data=_pdata, x="CategoriaFAS", y="y_score_ensemble",
               palette=_fas_palette, ax=ax, inner=None, alpha=0.4, cut=0)
sns.stripplot(data=_pdata, x="CategoriaFAS", y="y_score_ensemble",
              color="black", alpha=0.4, size=3, jitter=0.2, ax=ax)
# Group medians
for i, grp in enumerate(_fas_order):
    med = _pdata.loc[_pdata["CategoriaFAS"] == grp, "y_score_ensemble"].median()
    ax.hlines(med, i - 0.3, i + 0.3, colors="red", linewidths=2)
ax.set_xlabel("Fatigue Category")
ax.set_ylabel("AD-Likeness Score")
ax.set_title("A) AD-Likeness by Fatigue")
ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=15)

# Panel B: S_sig
ax = axes[1]
if "S_sig" in _pdata.columns and _pdata["S_sig"].notna().sum() > 10:
    sns.violinplot(data=_pdata, x="CategoriaFAS", y="S_sig",
                   palette=_fas_palette, ax=ax, inner=None, alpha=0.4, cut=0)
    sns.stripplot(data=_pdata, x="CategoriaFAS", y="S_sig",
                  color="black", alpha=0.4, size=3, jitter=0.2, ax=ax)
    ax.set_xlabel("Fatigue Category")
    ax.set_ylabel("AD Signature Score (S_sig)")
    ax.set_title("B) Signature Score by Fatigue")
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=15)
else:
    ax.text(0.5, 0.5, "S_sig not available", transform=ax.transAxes,
            ha="center", va="center")

# Panel C: Recon error
ax = axes[2]
if "recon_error_ensemble" in _pdata.columns:
    sns.violinplot(data=_pdata, x="CategoriaFAS", y="recon_error_ensemble",
                   palette=_fas_palette, ax=ax, inner=None, alpha=0.4, cut=0)
    sns.stripplot(data=_pdata, x="CategoriaFAS", y="recon_error_ensemble",
                  color="black", alpha=0.4, size=3, jitter=0.2, ax=ax)
    ax.set_xlabel("Fatigue Category")
    ax.set_ylabel("Reconstruction Error")
    ax.set_title("C) Recon Error by Fatigue")
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=15)

plt.suptitle("Model-derived metrics by fatigue group (COVID only)",
             fontsize=12, y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout()
save_fig(fig, "fig_fatigue_violin_main.png", close=False)
if SHOW_FIGURES: plt.show()
else: plt.close()

# ── Figure 2: Effect-size forest plot (extreme vs none) ──
_evn_path = TABLES_DIR / "fatigue_extreme_vs_none.csv"
if _evn_path.exists():
    _evn = pd.read_csv(_evn_path)
    if len(_evn) > 0:
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, max(4, len(_evn)*0.6)))
        y_pos = range(len(_evn))
        ax.errorbar(_evn["cliffs_delta"], y_pos,
                    xerr=[_evn["cliffs_delta"] - _evn["delta_CI_lo"],
                          _evn["delta_CI_hi"] - _evn["cliffs_delta"]],
                    fmt="o", color="#2C3E50", capsize=4, markersize=6)
        ax.set_yticks(list(y_pos))
        ax.set_yticklabels(_evn["variable"], fontsize=9)
        ax.axvline(0, color="grey", ls="--", lw=0.8)
        # Shade negligible zone
        ax.axvspan(-0.11, 0.11, alpha=0.08, color="grey")
        ax.set_xlabel("Cliff's δ (FATIGA EXTREMA − NO HAY FATIGA)")
        ax.set_title("Effect sizes: Extreme fatigue vs No fatigue\n"
                     "(shaded = negligible |δ| < 0.11)")
        ax.invert_yaxis()
        plt.tight_layout()
        save_fig(fig, "fig_fatigue_effect_sizes.png", close=False)
        if SHOW_FIGURES: plt.show()
        else: plt.close()

# ── Figure 3: Ordinal trend scatter ──
if "FAS_ordinal" in fat_valid.columns:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 5))
    _jitter = np.random.RandomState(SEED_GLOBAL).uniform(-0.15, 0.15, len(fat_valid))
    ax.scatter(fat_valid["FAS_ordinal"] + _jitter,
              fat_valid["y_score_ensemble"],
              c=[_fas_palette.get(c, "grey") for c in fat_valid["CategoriaFAS"]],
              s=25, alpha=0.6, edgecolors="none")

    # Group means with CI
    for i, grp in enumerate(_fas_order):
        vals = fat_valid.loc[fat_valid["CategoriaFAS"] == grp, "y_score_ensemble"].dropna()
        if len(vals) > 0:
            mu, lo, hi = bootstrap_ci(vals.values)
            ax.errorbar(i, mu, yerr=[[mu-lo], [hi-mu]], fmt="D",
                       color="black", markersize=8, capsize=5, zorder=10)

    ax.set_xticks([0, 1, 2])
    ax.set_xticklabels(_fas_order, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_xlabel("Fatigue Category (ordinal)")
    ax.set_ylabel("AD-Likeness Score")
    ax.set_title("AD-Likeness trend across fatigue burden\n(diamonds = group mean ± 95% CI)")
    plt.tight_layout()
    save_fig(fig, "fig_fatigue_ordinal_trend.png", close=False)
    if SHOW_FIGURES: plt.show()
    else: plt.close()

print("✓ Fatigue figures complete.")

✓ Fatigue figures complete.

§8 — Fatigue Analysis: Interpretation Summary¶

Within the tensor-restricted Long-COVID cohort, fatigue burden was strongly associated with reduced self-perceived health (EQ-VAS), confirming the clinical validity of the fatigue stratification. In contrast, the transferred AD-likeness score showed no robust association with fatigue category across omnibus tests, ordinal trend analysis, multivariable adjustment, or the extreme-vs-none contrast. Reconstruction error was likewise unrelated to fatigue burden. A weaker secondary signal emerged for the consensus-edge signature score (S_sig), which showed a positive ordinal trend and an association with fatigue in adjusted models; however, these effects did not survive the full multiple-testing framework and should therefore be treated as exploratory. Overall, the data do not support a strong relationship between fatigue burden and the transferred AD-likeness score in this cohort.

Sex confounding caveat. The fatigue groups show a marked sex imbalance (the "no fatigue" group is majority male whereas the "extreme fatigue" group is majority female). Because sex may independently affect both connectivity patterns and self-reported fatigue, any secondary signal (e.g., S_sig trend) could be partially or fully explained by this confound. The multivariable models in §8e include sex as a covariate, but with small stratum sizes the adjustment has limited statistical power. This imbalance should be considered when interpreting fatigue-related connectivity findings.

§9 — AD-Derived Signature Projection (S_sig)¶

$S_\text{sig} = \sum_{e} w_e \cdot z_e$ projects the ADNI-derived consensus edge weights onto each COVID subject's connectivity, where $z_e$ is CN-standardised (zero-mean, unit-variance relative to ADNI controls).

Scope. S_sig is used here only as a compact scalar summary of how much each subject's connectivity aligns with the AD-derived reference signature. It is not a validated COVID biomarker, not a mechanistic explanation, and not a substitute for proper COVID-specific analyses.

For anatomical decomposition of this signature (network-pair structure, ROI-level analysis, lateralization), see 06_further_analysis.ipynb.

In [23]:
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# §9  AD-Derived Signature Projection (S_sig) — scalar summary only
#   S_sig = Σ_e (w_signed_e · z_e),  z_e CN-standardised connectivity.
#   Treated as a compact scalar projection, NOT an anatomical biomarker.
#   Anatomical decomposition of the AD signature belongs in notebook 06.
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
if sig_df is not None:
    for clf in CLASSIFIER_TYPES:
        sub = ensemble[ensemble["classifier"] == clf].copy()
        sub = sub.merge(sig_df, on="SubjectID", how="left")

        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5))

        # Distribution
        ax = axes[0]
        ax.hist(sub["S_sig"].dropna(), bins=25, alpha=0.7, color="#8E44AD",
                edgecolor="white")
        mu_sig = sub["S_sig"].mean()
        ax.axvline(mu_sig, color="red", ls="--", label=f"mean={mu_sig:.2f}")
        ax.set_xlabel("AD Signature Score (S_sig)")
        ax.set_ylabel("Count")
        ax.set_title("Consensus-Edge Signature Distribution")
        ax.legend()

        # Score vs S_sig
        ax = axes[1]
        valid = sub.dropna(subset=["y_score_ensemble", "S_sig"])
        sc = ax.scatter(valid["y_score_ensemble"], valid["S_sig"],
                       c=valid["y_score_std"], cmap="viridis",
                       s=20, alpha=0.7, edgecolors="none")
        plt.colorbar(sc, ax=ax, label="Cross-fold σ")
        r_sig, p_sig = spearmanr(valid["y_score_ensemble"], valid["S_sig"])
        ax.text(0.02, 0.98, f"ρ={r_sig:.3f}, p={p_sig:.4f}",
                transform=ax.transAxes, va="top", fontsize=9,
                bbox=dict(facecolor="wheat", alpha=0.5))
        ax.set_xlabel("AD-Likeness Score")
        ax.set_ylabel("S_sig")
        ax.set_title(f"{clf.upper()} — Score vs Signature")

        plt.tight_layout()
        save_fig(fig, f"fig7_signature_score_{clf}.png", close=False)
        if SHOW_FIGURES: plt.show()
        else: plt.close()

        print(f"{clf.upper()} Signature: mean={mu_sig:.4f} ± {sub['S_sig'].std():.4f}")
        print(f"  ρ(score, S_sig) = {r_sig:.4f}, p = {p_sig:.4f}")

        # S_sig vs MOCA (if clinical data available)
        clin_path = TABLES_DIR / f"clinical_analysis_cohort_{clf}.csv"
        if clin_path.exists():
            c = clin_covid.copy()
            if "MOCA" in c.columns and "S_sig" in c.columns:
                v = c[["S_sig", "MOCA"]].dropna()
                if len(v) > 10:
                    r_m, p_m = spearmanr(v["S_sig"], v["MOCA"])
                    print(f"  ρ(S_sig, MOCA) = {r_m:.4f}, p = {p_m:.4f}")
else:
    print("⚠ No signature scores available. Skipping §9.")

LOGREG Signature: mean=-6.8960 ± 11.2546
  ρ(score, S_sig) = 0.0830, p = 0.2498
  ρ(S_sig, MOCA) = -0.1369, p = 0.0938

§10 — Pathological Orthogonality: COVID-vs-Control Global Enrichment¶

Question. Does the Long-COVID vs Control connectomic contrast overlap with the AD-derived reference signature more than expected by chance?

This is the only edge-level connectomic test retained in this notebook. It is externally defensible because:

  1. Groups are defined by study design (COVID vs Control), not by the transferred score.
  2. The AD-derived signature edges are an independent reference set (from ADNI interpretability), not trained on COVID data.
  3. The test measures biological overlap between two independently derived contrasts, not circular self-confirmation.

Method. Mann–Whitney U per edge on all 8 515 upper-triangle edges (not restricted to signature edges). The top 5 % by |Cliff's δ| form the "hit set". Fisher's exact test measures overlap with the 1 026 AD-signature edges. Permutation validation confirms analytic p-values.

Channel-specific caveat. Enrichment is computed per channel and the results may differ substantially across connectivity measures. A strong enrichment in one channel (e.g., OMST graph-based connectivity) does not imply enrichment in others (e.g., full Pearson or MI). Interpret any significant overlap as specific to the connectivity measure that produced it, not as a global connectomic statement.

Important. This test does not prove that COVID "has AD". It asks whether the connectivity differences between Long-COVID and Controls happen to occur in the same edges that distinguish AD from CN — which would suggest shared network vulnerability rather than shared pathology.

Within-COVID edge testing (splitting subjects by transferred AD-likeness score, then testing edges) has been removed as circular. See changelog.

In [24]:
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# §10  Pathological Orthogonality: Long-COVID vs CONTROL enrichment
#   COVID-vs-Control is the only defensible edge-level comparison here:
#   independent groups, not derived from the transferred score.
#   Enrichment computed on ALL 8515 upper-triangle edges (not restricted
#   to 1026 signature edges). Pre-computed by analysis_covid_paper_fixes.py
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
from scipy.stats import binomtest

print("="*70)
print("§10 — Pathological Orthogonality: Long-COVID vs Study Controls")
print("       + Full-universe enrichment (8515 edges)")
print("="*70)

# ── Map consensus edges to ROI integer indices ───────────────────────
def _normalize_roi(s):
    """Lower-case, strip punctuation/whitespace for fuzzy ROI matching."""
    import re as _re
    s = s.casefold()
    s = _re.sub(r"[\(\)\[\]\{\},;:'\".]+", " ", s)
    s = _re.sub(r"[_\-]+", " ", s)
    s = _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s)
    return s.strip()

_roi_info = pd.read_csv(RESULTS_DIR / "roi_info_from_tensor.csv")
_roi_col = "roi_name_in_tensor"
_roi_info["_norm"] = _roi_info[_roi_col].map(_normalize_roi)
_roi2idx = {n: i for i, n in enumerate(_roi_info["_norm"].values)}

_ce = consensus_edges.copy()
_ce["_src_norm"] = _ce["src_AAL3_Name"].map(_normalize_roi)
_ce["_dst_norm"] = _ce["dst_AAL3_Name"].map(_normalize_roi)
_ce["src_idx"] = _ce["_src_norm"].map(_roi2idx)
_ce["dst_idx"] = _ce["_dst_norm"].map(_roi2idx)

_unmapped = _ce["src_idx"].isna() | _ce["dst_idx"].isna()
_n_mapped = (~_unmapped).sum()
_map_rate = _n_mapped / len(_ce) * 100
print(f"  Edge mapping: {_n_mapped}/{len(_ce)} ({_map_rate:.1f}%)")
assert _map_rate >= 95, f"Edge mapping rate {_map_rate:.1f}% < 95%"

consensus_edges_mapped = _ce.loc[~_unmapped].copy()
consensus_edges_mapped["src_idx"] = consensus_edges_mapped["src_idx"].astype(int)
consensus_edges_mapped["dst_idx"] = consensus_edges_mapped["dst_idx"].astype(int)
_a = consensus_edges_mapped["src_idx"].values
_b = consensus_edges_mapped["dst_idx"].values
consensus_edges_mapped["i"] = np.minimum(_a, _b)
consensus_edges_mapped["j"] = np.maximum(_a, _b)
print(f"  consensus_edges_mapped: {len(consensus_edges_mapped)} edges")

# ── Group masks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_covid_set = set(clin_covid["SubjectID"].astype(str))
_ctrl_set  = set(clin_control["SubjectID"].astype(str))
_mask_covid = np.array([sid in _covid_set for sid in covid_ids])
_mask_ctrl  = np.array([sid in _ctrl_set  for sid in covid_ids])

_n1 = _mask_covid.sum()
_n2 = _mask_ctrl.sum()
assert _n1 >= 10 and _n2 >= 10, f"Insufficient sizes: COVID={_n1}, CTRL={_n2}"
print(f"  Long-COVID: n = {_n1}")
print(f"  Controls:   n = {_n2}")

_ce = consensus_edges_mapped
_ei = _ce["i"].values
_ej = _ce["j"].values

# AD-signature edge set
_sig_edge_set = set(zip(_ei, _ej))

for ch_idx in channels_to_use:
    _ch_name = covid_chs[ch_idx] if ch_idx < len(covid_chs) else f"ch{ch_idx}"
    print(f"\n  ── Channel {ch_idx} ({_ch_name[:50]}) ──")
    Xch = covid_tensor[:, ch_idx, :, :]

    # ── Signature-edge tests (display) ───────────────────────────
    v1 = Xch[_mask_covid][:, _ei, _ej]
    v2 = Xch[_mask_ctrl][:, _ei, _ej]

    _n_e = v1.shape[1]
    _p = np.empty(_n_e)
    _delta = np.empty(_n_e)

    for k in range(_n_e):
        _u, _pv = mannwhitneyu(v1[:, k], v2[:, k],
                               alternative="two-sided", method="asymptotic")
        _p[k] = _pv
        _delta[k] = 2 * _u / (_n1 * _n2) - 1

    _, _q, _, _ = multipletests(_p, method="fdr_bh")

    _out = pd.DataFrame({
        "src_AAL3_Name": _ce["src_AAL3_Name"].values,
        "dst_AAL3_Name": _ce["dst_AAL3_Name"].values,
        "i": _ei, "j": _ej, "ch_idx": ch_idx,
        "p_raw": _p, "q_fdr": _q, "cliffs_delta": _delta,
        "mean_COVID": v1.mean(axis=0), "mean_CONTROL": v2.mean(axis=0),
        "abs_delta": np.abs(_delta),
    })
    _out["sig_fdr05"] = _out["q_fdr"] < 0.05
    _out = _out.sort_values("abs_delta", ascending=False)
    _out.to_csv(TABLES_DIR / f"covid_vs_control_signature_edges_ch{ch_idx}.csv",
                index=False)

    _n_sig = _out["sig_fdr05"].sum()
    print(f"    Signature edges FDR<0.05: {_n_sig} / {_n_e}")
    print(f"    |Cliff's δ| — median: {np.median(np.abs(_delta)):.4f}, "
          f"max: {np.max(np.abs(_delta)):.4f}")

    # ── Full-universe enrichment (pre-computed) ──────────────────
    _enrich_path = TABLES_DIR / f"enrichment_signature_vs_top5pct_universe_ch{ch_idx}.csv"
    if _enrich_path.exists():
        _enrich = pd.read_csv(_enrich_path)
        _r1 = _enrich[_enrich["test"] == "top5pct_by_abs_delta_UNIVERSE"].iloc[0]
        print(f"    Full-universe enrichment (top 5% |δ| of 8515 ∩ AD-sig):")
        print(f"      overlap={int(_r1['overlap'])}/{int(_r1['n_hit'])}, "
              f"OR={_r1['odds_ratio']:.2f}, p_Fisher={_r1['p_fisher']:.4g}")
        print(f"      Permutation (n={int(_r1['n_perm'])}): "
              f"null mean={_r1['perm_mean_overlap']:.1f}±{_r1['perm_std_overlap']:.1f}, "
              f"p_perm={_r1['p_perm']:.4g}")
    else:
        print(f"    ⚠ Full-universe enrichment not found. "
              f"Run: python scripts/analysis_covid_paper_fixes.py")

    # ── Figure: effect summary + p-histogram (2 panels) ──────────
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5.5))

    # (a) Effect-size density
    ax = axes[0]
    ax.hist(_delta, bins=40, color="#7F8C8D", edgecolor="white", alpha=0.85)
    ax.axvline(0, color="black", lw=0.8, ls="--")
    _med = np.median(_delta)
    ax.axvline(_med, color="#E74C3C", lw=1.2,
               label=f"median δ = {_med:+.4f}")
    ax.set_xlabel("Cliff's δ  (COVID − Control)")
    ax.set_ylabel("Number of edges")
    ax.set_title(f"Effect-size distribution — ch{ch_idx}")
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)

    # (b) Raw p-value histogram (uniform under H0)
    ax = axes[1]
    ax.hist(_p, bins=20, color="#3498DB", edgecolor="white", alpha=0.8)
    ax.axhline(len(_p) / 20, color="red", ls="--", lw=0.8,
               label="Uniform expectation")
    ax.set_xlabel("Raw p-value")
    ax.set_ylabel("Count")
    ax.set_title(f"p-value histogram — ch{ch_idx}")
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)

    fig.suptitle(
        f"§10 — Long-COVID (n={_n1}) vs Controls (n={_n2}), "
        f"channel {ch_idx}",
        fontsize=11, y=1.02)
    plt.tight_layout()
    save_fig(fig, f"fig10_covid_vs_control_edges_ch{ch_idx}.png", close=False)
    if SHOW_FIGURES: plt.show()
    else: plt.close()

======================================================================
§10 — Pathological Orthogonality: Long-COVID vs Study Controls
       + Full-universe enrichment (8515 edges)
======================================================================
  Edge mapping: 1026/1026 (100.0%)
  consensus_edges_mapped: 1026 edges
  Long-COVID: n = 151
  Controls:   n = 43

  ── Channel 1 (Pearson_Full_FisherZ_Signed) ──
    Signature edges FDR<0.05: 0 / 1026
    |Cliff's δ| — median: 0.0682, max: 0.3002
    Full-universe enrichment (top 5% |δ| of 8515 ∩ AD-sig):
      overlap=49/425, OR=0.95, p_Fisher=0.6553
      Permutation (n=5000): null mean=51.0±6.5, p_perm=0.6512

  ── Channel 0 (Pearson_OMST_GCE_Signed_Weighted) ──
    Signature edges FDR<0.05: 0 / 1026
    |Cliff's δ| — median: 0.0279, max: 0.2398
    Full-universe enrichment (top 5% |δ| of 8515 ∩ AD-sig):
      overlap=126/425, OR=3.37, p_Fisher=1.191e-23
      Permutation (n=5000): null mean=51.0±6.5, p_perm=0

  ── Channel 2 (MI_KNN_Symmetric) ──
    Signature edges FDR<0.05: 0 / 1026
    |Cliff's δ| — median: 0.0658, max: 0.3150
    Full-universe enrichment (top 5% |δ| of 8515 ∩ AD-sig):
      overlap=53/425, OR=1.04, p_Fisher=0.4153
      Permutation (n=5000): null mean=51.0±6.5, p_perm=0.398

Appendix A — Calibration Drift & Sensitivity¶

Compare COVID score distribution to ADNI CN and AD out-of-fold distributions. The COVID scores are not diagnostic probabilities — they are AD-likeness indices whose calibration may not transfer.

Sensitivity analysis: repeat key clinical associations under multiple ADNI-derived thresholds and OOD gating.

In [25]:
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# §App-A  Score distribution shift overlay (ADNI OOF vs COVID groups)
#   Fix: do NOT label COVID (n=194) when it includes CONTROL+COVID.
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

from scipy.stats import ks_2samp

assert adni_oof_all is not None, "Need ADNI OOF predictions for overlay."

adni_oof = adni_oof_all[adni_oof_all["classifier_type"] == TARGET_CLF].copy()
adni_cn = adni_oof.loc[adni_oof["y_true"] == 0, "y_score_final"].values
adni_ad = adni_oof.loc[adni_oof["y_true"] == 1, "y_score_final"].values

# Build group-aware COVID scores
meta_min = covid_meta_raw[["_merge_id", "ResearchGroup"]].copy()
meta_min["ResearchGroup"] = meta_min["ResearchGroup"].astype(str)

for clf in CLASSIFIER_TYPES:
    sub = ensemble[ensemble["classifier"] == clf].copy()
    sub = sub.merge(meta_min, left_on="SubjectID", right_on="_merge_id", how="left")

    # Defensive: ensure group is present
    if sub["ResearchGroup"].isna().any():
        missing_ids = sub.loc[sub["ResearchGroup"].isna(), "SubjectID"].unique()[:10]
        print(f"⚠ Missing ResearchGroup for {sub['ResearchGroup'].isna().sum()} subjects. Examples: {missing_ids}")

    scores_all = sub["y_score_ensemble"].values
    scores_covid = sub.loc[sub["ResearchGroup"] == "COVID", "y_score_ensemble"].values
    scores_ctrl  = sub.loc[sub["ResearchGroup"] == "CONTROL", "y_score_ensemble"].values

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
    bins = np.linspace(0, 1, 40)

    # ADNI references
    ax.hist(adni_cn, bins=bins, alpha=0.25, density=True,
            label=f"ADNI CN (OOF, n={len(adni_cn)})")
    ax.hist(adni_ad, bins=bins, alpha=0.25, density=True,
            label=f"ADNI AD (OOF, n={len(adni_ad)})")

    # COVID-study groups (correct labels)
    ax.hist(scores_covid, bins=bins, alpha=0.35, density=True,
            label=f"Long-COVID (n={len(scores_covid)})")
    ax.hist(scores_ctrl, bins=bins, alpha=0.35, density=True,
            label=f"Controls (n={len(scores_ctrl)})")

    # Optional: total cohort line (NOT called COVID)
    kde_all = gaussian_kde(scores_all, bw_method=0.2)
    xs = np.linspace(0, 1, 300)
    ax.plot(xs, kde_all(xs), color="grey", lw=1.5, ls="--",
            label=f"All study cohort (COVID+CONTROL, n={len(scores_all)})")

    # Threshold markers
    for name, d in threshold_policies.items():
        t = d["threshold"]
        ax.axvline(t, color="grey", ls=":", lw=0.8, alpha=0.7)

    ax.set_xlabel("Score (AD-likeness)")
    ax.set_ylabel("Density")
    ax.set_title(f"Score Distribution Shift: ADNI OOF vs COVID-study groups ({clf.upper()})")
    ax.set_xlim(0, 1)
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    plt.tight_layout()
    save_fig(fig, f"fig10_score_shift_overlay_{clf}.png", close=False)
    if SHOW_FIGURES: plt.show()
    else: plt.close()

    # KS tests (report separately)
    def _ks(a, b, name_a, name_b):
        ks, p = ks_2samp(a, b)
        print(f"  KS({name_a}, {name_b}) = {ks:.4f}, p={p:.3e}")

    print(f"\n{clf.upper()} — Distribution shift (KS tests):")
    _ks(scores_covid, adni_cn, "Long-COVID", "ADNI-CN")
    _ks(scores_covid, adni_ad, "Long-COVID", "ADNI-AD")
    _ks(scores_ctrl,  adni_cn, "Controls",   "ADNI-CN")
    _ks(scores_ctrl,  adni_ad, "Controls",   "ADNI-AD")
    _ks(scores_covid, scores_ctrl, "Long-COVID", "Controls")

LOGREG — Distribution shift (KS tests):
  KS(Long-COVID, ADNI-CN) = 0.1284, p=2.834e-01
  KS(Long-COVID, ADNI-AD) = 0.6429, p=3.967e-23
  KS(Controls, ADNI-CN) = 0.2211, p=9.714e-02
  KS(Controls, ADNI-AD) = 0.6338, p=9.367e-12
  KS(Long-COVID, Controls) = 0.1446, p=4.366e-01
In [26]:
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# §App-A  Sensitivity analysis: MOCA × threshold on Long-COVID + Controls
#   Primary: Long-COVID only.  Secondary: Study controls (robustness).
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
from scipy.stats import spearmanr as _spearmanr_sa

_sens_rows = []

# Resolve cohorts from globals (guard against NameError)
_sa_all = clin_all.copy() if "clin_all" in dir() else _clin_df.copy()
_sa_all["ResearchGroup"] = (
    _sa_all["ResearchGroup"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()
)
_sa_covid = _sa_all[_sa_all["ResearchGroup"] == "COVID"].copy()
_sa_ctrl  = _sa_all[_sa_all["ResearchGroup"] == "CONTROL"].copy()

for policy_name, pol in threshold_policies.items():
    thr = pol["threshold"]
    for cohort_label, cohort_df in [("Long-COVID", _sa_covid),
                                     ("Controls", _sa_ctrl),
                                     ("All", _sa_all)]:
        moca_valid = cohort_df[cohort_df["MOCA"].notna()].copy()
        if len(moca_valid) < 10:
            continue
        above = moca_valid[moca_valid["y_score_ensemble"] >= thr]
        below = moca_valid[moca_valid["y_score_ensemble"] < thr]
        r, p = _spearmanr_sa(moca_valid["y_score_ensemble"], moca_valid["MOCA"])
        _sens_rows.append({
            "policy": policy_name, "threshold": thr, "cohort": cohort_label,
            "n_total": len(moca_valid),
            "n_above": len(above), "n_below": len(below),
            "moca_above_median": above["MOCA"].median() if len(above) > 0 else np.nan,
            "moca_below_median": below["MOCA"].median() if len(below) > 0 else np.nan,
            "moca_above_mean": above["MOCA"].mean() if len(above) > 0 else np.nan,
            "moca_below_mean": below["MOCA"].mean() if len(below) > 0 else np.nan,
            "spearman_rho": r, "spearman_p": p,
        })

if _sens_rows:
    _sens_df = pd.DataFrame(_sens_rows)
    # Save combined
    _sens_df.to_csv(TABLES_DIR / "sensitivity_moca_by_threshold.csv", index=False)
    # Save Long-COVID separately
    _sens_covid = _sens_df[_sens_df["cohort"] == "Long-COVID"]
    _sens_covid.to_csv(
        TABLES_DIR / "sensitivity_moca_by_threshold_covid.csv", index=False)
    # Save Controls separately
    _sens_ctrl_df = _sens_df[_sens_df["cohort"] == "Controls"]
    if len(_sens_ctrl_df) > 0:
        _sens_ctrl_df.to_csv(
            TABLES_DIR / "sensitivity_moca_by_threshold_controls.csv", index=False)

    print("Sensitivity Analysis: MOCA by threshold policy\n")
    print("  PRIMARY (Long-COVID only):")
    for _, r in _sens_covid.iterrows():
        above_med = f"{r['moca_above_median']:.1f}" if not np.isnan(r['moca_above_median']) else "n/a"
        below_med = f"{r['moca_below_median']:.1f}" if not np.isnan(r['moca_below_median']) else "n/a"
        print(f"    {r['policy']:12s} (t={r['threshold']:.4f}): "
              f"above n={r['n_above']:3.0f} MOCA median={above_med}, "
              f"below n={r['n_below']:3.0f} MOCA median={below_med}, "
              f"ρ={r['spearman_rho']:.3f}")
    if len(_sens_ctrl_df) > 0:
        print("\n  SECONDARY (Controls — robustness check):")
        for _, r in _sens_ctrl_df.iterrows():
            above_med = f"{r['moca_above_median']:.1f}" if not np.isnan(r['moca_above_median']) else "n/a"
            below_med = f"{r['moca_below_median']:.1f}" if not np.isnan(r['moca_below_median']) else "n/a"
            print(f"    {r['policy']:12s} (t={r['threshold']:.4f}): "
                  f"above n={r['n_above']:3.0f} MOCA median={above_med}, "
                  f"below n={r['n_below']:3.0f} MOCA median={below_med}, "
                  f"ρ={r['spearman_rho']:.3f}")
else:
    print("  ⚠ No data for sensitivity analysis.")

Sensitivity Analysis: MOCA by threshold policy

  PRIMARY (Long-COVID only):
    Youden       (t=0.4935): above n= 35 MOCA median=26.0, below n=116 MOCA median=27.0, ρ=-0.078
    Screening    (t=0.4935): above n= 35 MOCA median=26.0, below n=116 MOCA median=27.0, ρ=-0.078
    FixedFPR     (t=0.6619): above n=  4 MOCA median=26.5, below n=147 MOCA median=26.0, ρ=-0.078
    CostBased    (t=0.4122): above n= 59 MOCA median=26.0, below n= 92 MOCA median=27.0, ρ=-0.078

  SECONDARY (Controls — robustness check):
    Youden       (t=0.4935): above n= 10 MOCA median=26.5, below n= 33 MOCA median=27.0, ρ=0.174
    Screening    (t=0.4935): above n= 10 MOCA median=26.5, below n= 33 MOCA median=27.0, ρ=0.174
    FixedFPR     (t=0.6619): above n=  2 MOCA median=23.5, below n= 41 MOCA median=27.0, ρ=0.174
    CostBased    (t=0.4122): above n= 13 MOCA median=27.0, below n= 30 MOCA median=26.5, ρ=0.174

§F — Outputs Index & Provenance¶

In [27]:
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# §F  OUTPUTS INDEX + MANIFEST
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── Expected outputs from notebook v6.1 ──────────────────────────
_EXPECTED_PREFIXES = {
    # §4
    "fig1_score_distribution_", "fig2_cross_fold_stability_",
    # §5
    "fig3_ood_diagnostics_",
    # §6
    "fig4_threshold_policy_", "adni_derived_thresholds",
    # §7
    "fig5_clinical_correlations", "fig6_clinical_boxplots",
    "clinical_analysis_cohort_", "clinical_correlations",
    "clinical_group_comparisons",
    # §8
    "fatigue_", "fig_fatigue_",
    # §9
    "fig7_signature_score_",
    # §10
    "fig10_covid_vs_control_edges_", "covid_vs_control_signature_edges_",
    "enrichment_signature_vs_top5pct_universe_",
    # App-A
    "fig10_score_shift_overlay_", "sensitivity_moca_by_threshold",
    # §2 inference tables
    "covid_predictions_", "covid_recon_error_", "covid_latent_distance_",
    "covid_signature_scores", "covid_ood_quadrants_",
    "covid_threshold_analysis_", "covid_fold_stability_",
    # OOD
    "adni_ood_distribution", "ood_policy_sensitivity_summary",
    # Manifest
    "notebook_manifest", "run_manifest", "outputs_index",
}

def _is_expected(fname):
    """Check if a filename matches any expected prefix."""
    return any(fname.startswith(p) for p in _EXPECTED_PREFIXES)

# ── Build manifest ───────────────────────────────────────────────
nb_manifest = {
    "created_utc": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
    "notebook_version": NOTEBOOK_VERSION,
    "python_version": sys.version,
    "numpy_version": np.__version__,
    "pandas_version": pd.__version__,
    "project_root": str(PROJECT_ROOT),
    "results_dir": str(RESULTS_DIR),
    "covid_tensor": str(covid_tp),
    "covid_metadata": str(covid_meta_path),
    "n_covid_subjects": int(N_COVID),
    "classifier_types": CLASSIFIER_TYPES,
    "target_clf": TARGET_CLF,
    "threshold_policies": {k: v["threshold"] for k, v in threshold_policies.items()},
    "seed_global": SEED_GLOBAL,
}

# Git hash (best effort)
try:
    git_hash = subprocess.check_output(
        ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"], cwd=str(PROJECT_ROOT),
        stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL).decode().strip()
    nb_manifest["git_hash"] = git_hash
except Exception:
    nb_manifest["git_hash"] = "unavailable"

manifest_nb_path = OUTPUT_DIR / "notebook_manifest.json"
with open(manifest_nb_path, "w") as f:
    json.dump(nb_manifest, f, indent=2)
print(f"Manifest → {manifest_nb_path}")

# ── Outputs index ────────────────────────────────────────────────
index_rows = []
for subdir in ["Tables", "Figures", "Logs"]:
    d = OUTPUT_DIR / subdir
    if d.exists():
        for fp in sorted(d.iterdir()):
            if fp.is_file():
                index_rows.append({
                    "file": fp.name, "folder": subdir,
                    "size_kb": round(fp.stat().st_size / 1024, 1),
                    "modified": pd.Timestamp(fp.stat().st_mtime, unit="s")
                              .strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"),
                    "status": "current" if _is_expected(fp.stem) else "legacy",
                })
for fp in OUTPUT_DIR.glob("*.json"):
    index_rows.append({
        "file": fp.name, "folder": ".",
        "size_kb": round(fp.stat().st_size / 1024, 1),
        "modified": pd.Timestamp(fp.stat().st_mtime, unit="s")
                  .strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"),
        "status": "current" if _is_expected(fp.stem) else "legacy",
    })
if index_rows:
    idx_df = pd.DataFrame(index_rows)
    idx_df.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "outputs_index.csv", index=False)
    n_current = (idx_df["status"] == "current").sum()
    n_legacy  = (idx_df["status"] == "legacy").sum()
    print(f"\nOutputs index ({len(idx_df)} files: {n_current} current, {n_legacy} legacy):")
    print(idx_df.to_string(index=False))
else:
    print("No outputs found.")

Manifest → /home/diego/proyectos/vae_AD/results/vae_3channels_beta65_pro/inference_covid_paper_output/notebook_manifest.json

                   covid_subject_selection_logreg.csv  Tables      3.3 2026-03-11 19:55  legacy
                  covid_threshold_analysis_logreg.csv  Tables     23.1 2026-03-11 21:48 current
             covid_vs_control_all_edges_tests_ch0.csv  Tables    972.6 2026-03-05 12:21  legacy
             covid_vs_control_all_edges_tests_ch1.csv  Tables   1183.9 2026-03-05 12:21  legacy
